# Records of the !mmortal Data Scientist

> Machine learning research notes by Taha Bouhsine — neural network interpretability, kernel methods, contrastive learning, attention mechanisms, RKHS, and transformer architectures. Long-form pieces with interactive visualisations.

Long-form machine-learning research notes by Taha Bouhsine (Machine Learning Researcher). Every post is built around live, in-browser interactive visualizations: the math actually runs in the page, not as pre-rendered figures. Recurring threads: neural-network interpretability, kernel methods and RKHS, contrastive learning, attention mechanisms, the Welch bound and simplex/ETF geometry, and white-box architectures.

## Posts

- [The Hand-Built Network, in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/handbuilt-features-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-24): Build the hand-built Fashion-MNIST classifier in JAX/Flax NNX: a pure-JAX HOG-style feature extractor and a constructed Yat head, 83.3% with zero training.
- [You Don't Even Have to Train the Features](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/you-dont-have-to-train-the-features/) (2026-06-24): Hand-built edge and corner detectors plus a constructed Yat head classify Fashion-MNIST at 83.3% with zero training anywhere, matching a trained backbone.
- [You Only Have to Train the Features](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/train-the-features/) (2026-06-23): A Yat head built on learned features hits 83.2% vs 85.7% trained, and 74% on a random backbone. The accuracy is in the representation, not the classifier.
- [Constructing the Head on Learned Features, in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/train-the-features-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-23): Train a Flax NNX conv backbone, build a constructed Yat head on its features with no training: 85.9% vs 87.7%, and 73% on a random backbone whose trained head is at chance.
- [Editing a Network by Hand, in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/edit-a-network-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-22): Build a Yat-kernel MLP in Flax NNX, then add a class with jnp.concatenate and forget one with a boolean mask, all with no training.
- [Your Network Is a List of Pictures. You Can Edit It.](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/edit-a-network-by-hand/) (2026-06-22): A Yat-kernel network is a list of prototype pictures you can edit. Add a class with no training (95% recall), or delete one to unlearn it exactly.
- [Your Neuron Is a Picture, in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/yat-mlp-fmnist-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-21): Build a Yat-kernel MLP on Fashion-MNIST in Flax NNX: prototypes as images, prediction as a vote over pictures, OOD abstention, and UMAP prototype paths.
- [Your Neuron Is a Direction. It Should Be a Picture.](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/your-neuron-is-a-picture/) (2026-06-21): A direction tells you which way; a prototype tells you which thing. A Yat-kernel MLP on Fashion-MNIST where each neuron is a picture that explains itself.
- [The Yat-Kernel MLP in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/yat-mlp-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-18): The Yat-kernel MLP in JAX/Flax NNX: a layer with no activation function. Build it, assert PD + nonnegativity, the exact feature map, and train it on two moons.
- [What a Finite Kernel Buys an MLP](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/what-a-finite-kernel-buys-an-mlp/) (2026-06-18): Replacing the MLP activation with the finite, positive-definite Yat kernel turns it into a kernel machine: locality, attribution, geometry, capacity control.
- [Self-Attention as Kernel Regression in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/attention-is-kernel-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-15): Self-attention as Nadaraya–Watson kernel regression in JAX/Flax NNX: build attention from scratch, assert the equivalence in code, then swap the kernel.
- [The Three States of Information, in JAX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/three-states-of-information-jax/) (2026-06-07): Three states of information in JAX: effective rank, simplicity bias, neural-collapse equiangularity, and contrastive alignment versus uniformity.
- [Latent on the Spectrum, in JAX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/latent-on-the-spectrum-jax/) (2026-06-07): Spectral codebooks in JAX: classical MDS, flat-to-simplex and graded-to-horseshoe spectra, kernel-target alignment, and the between/within-class split.
- [Latent on the Spectrum: Why Cats Sit Closer to Dogs Than to Cars](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/latent-on-the-spectrum/) (2026-06-07): A latent space is a finite encoding of a label-similarity kernel: its codebook is the top eigenmodes of that matrix, and the Welch bound sets the rest.
- [The Three States of Information](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/three-states-of-information/) (2026-06-07): Representations pass through three states while training (random, organized, structured), and the transitions are the loss plateaus. With live training viz.
- [Q and K Projections in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/qk-projections-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-04): Q and K projections in JAX/Flax NNX: pull B=W_QW_Kᵀ from the weights, split it into a metric and a directed part, add RoPE, check the rank budget.
- [Why Attention Needs Q and K Projections](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/why-attention-needs-qk-projections/) (2026-06-04): Q and K projections turn attention's dot product into a learned, low-rank bilinear form x_iᵀW_QW_Kᵀx_j: directional, role-aware, and different per head.
- [The Prototype Readout in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/convex-readout-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-06-04): Prototype readout in JAX/Flax NNX: W_out as output prototypes, the convex/conic/affine/linear regimes, and a kernel readout that is convex by construction.
- [The Readout is a Convex Combination of Prototypes](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/readout-as-convex-combination/) (2026-06-04): The transformer MLP readout is a weighted sum over output prototypes (the columns of W_out); nonnegative, normalized coefficients make it a convex combination.
- [Auditing Latent Space Geometry in JAX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/welch-bound-jax-analysis/) (2026-06-02): A runnable JAX companion to the Welch-bound post: metrics that tell you whether embeddings collapse, waste rank, form a simplex, or press the Welch floor.
- [What Makes a Good Latent Space? The Welch Bound and the Simplex](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/welch-bound-good-latent-space/) (2026-06-02): Why class means form a simplex, and why the Welch bound from radio engineering gives the best latent geometry when too many concepts share too few dimensions.
- [Cheap Attention in JAX/Flax NNX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/linear-attention-jax-flax-nnx/) (2026-05-31): Positive-feature linear attention in JAX and Flax NNX: turn the all-pairs softmax ledger into a shared feature state where the N×N matrix never forms.
- [Cheap Attention: Linear-Time Kernel Approximation](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/cheap-attention-is-linear-attention/) (2026-05-31): Softmax attention is a kernel; approximate its feature map with random features and the N×N matrix becomes an O(N) shared state. Linear attention with live viz.
- [Organizing Randomness: Contrastive Learning in JAX](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/organizing-randomness-jax/) (2026-05-29): Six contrastive losses in JAX and Optax, each an animated GIF turning random 2D points into organized embeddings. Companion to Untangling the Moons.
- [Untangling the Moons: A Visual History of Contrastive Learning](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/untangling-the-moons/) (2026-05-26): Eight contrastive losses (pair, triplet, InfoNCE, CLIP, SupCon, SigLIP, alignment+uniformity, cosine) organizing 2D points in one interactive playground.
- [What an MLP Knows, When It's a Kernel](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/what-an-mlp-knows/) (2026-05-21): The transformer MLP is illegible because its primitive carries no kernel. Give it one and the objects that make attention legible follow, network-wide.
- [Attention is Explainable Because it is a Kernel](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/attention-is-a-kernel/) (2026-05-14): Self-attention is a Nadaraya-Watson kernel smoother. That fact, not the plottable matrix, is why attention heads are readable while MLPs are not.
- [Not All Infinities Are Equal: The Cross-Entropy Asymmetry Behind Hallucination](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/not-all-infinities-are-equal/) (2026-02-23): Cross-entropy's singularity structure is asymmetric, and that explains LLM hallucination, the CLIP modality gap, and why contrastive losses need huge batches.
- [Opposite Is Not Different: The Cosine-Similarity Bug in CLIP and Contrastive Learning](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/opposite-is-not-different/) (2026-02-22): Maximum difference between unit vectors is orthogonality (cos 0), not opposition (cos -1). CLIP, InfoNCE, and SimCLR optimize the wrong target.
- [Activations Are Bad for Geometry](https://tahabouhsine.com/blog/activations-are-bad-for-geometry/) (2026-02-20): ReLU and GELU enter a layer's Jacobian as a diagonal modulation that wrecks the data manifold's geometry. Pointwise activations as a representational bug.
